body.title: UC-side delivery telemetry — trace POST → fanout → per-WS-client send
body.result: 7 log points + zero-fanout channel diagnostic across UC POST handler, agent_send, broadcast, enqueue exceptions, WS send failures, join_session, disconnect. Next drop will announce its stage. 11 tests. db3ce4ab.
body.description:
Igor-side send telemetry (d5186515) catches Igor→UC failures, but the observed drops (09:58:26 804c5f99, 08:27:19 fb274171) return 200 OK and vanish downstream. Log each stage on UC so drop-point is localizable.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/utility_closet_server.py
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — logging + channel diagnostics at 7 points (POST entry, fanout count, enqueue exceptions, ws.send_text failures, join_session, disconnect, zero-fanout channel post). OUT — fixing the suspected session_id mismatch itself (separate ticket if/when logs confirm it).
**Test plan:** unit tests for _broadcast_to_session (count logging + zero-fanout diagnostic), agent_send (log on entry), and mock-WS _send_text failure (log on exception). Channel-post is lazy-imported (same pattern as d5186515) to keep the server testable without Igor DB.

Principal hypothesis from stage-walk of the delivery path: the web browser joined a session_id different from the one Igor sends to (default 'shared'). _broadcast_to_session looks up _session_clients[session_id]; if empty, fanout is a silent no-op and agent POST still returns 200. The zero-fanout diagnostic will prove or falsify this immediately on the next drop.
